sections.paragraph:
ἀξίως adv. fr. ἄξιος (Soph., Hdt.+; ins, pap, LXX; ApcEsdr 2:20 p. 26, 12 Tdf.; EpArist 32:39; Just.; Tat. 7, 2) worthily, in a manner worthy of, suitably w. gen. in ref. to a deity or person τοῦ θεοῦ (Hyperid. 3:25 τῆς θεοῦ; Just., A I, 15, 5, D. 58, 2) 1 Th 2:12; 3J 6; cp. Pol 5:2; 1 Cl 21:1. τοῦ κυρίου Col 1:10 (on these formulas, which have many counterparts in ins relating to publicspirited pers., s. Dssm. NB 75f [BS 248f]; Nägeli 54; Thieme 21; their use in connection with ordinary Christians suggests an extraordinary status in the new order). τῶν ἁγίων Ro 16:2. ὑπηρετῶν Dg 11:1. W. gen. of thing (Diod S 1, 51, 7 τῆς ἀληθείας ἀ.; Appian, Bell. Civ. 5, 36 §146 ἀ. τῆς ἀνάγκης=in a manner corresponding to the necessity; Wsd 7:15) τοῦ εὐαγγελίου Phil 1:27. τῆς κλήσεως Eph 4:1. τῆς ἐντολῆς Pol 5:1. Without such a gen. ἀγαπᾶν τινα ἀ. love someone in a suitable manner (=‘as the pers. deserves’; cp. Thu. 3, 40, 8 κολάσατε ἀξίως τούτους. Wsd 16:1; Sir 14:11; Tat. 7, 2) MPol 17:3.—M-M. s.v. ἄξιος.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

  1. fr. = from

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

gen. gen. = genitive

ref. ref. = reference(s)

Hyperid Hyperid , IV b.c.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

pers. pers. = person(s)

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

BS BS , s. Dssm., BS—List 6

Thieme Thieme = GT., Die Inschriften von Magnesia am Mäander und das NT—List 3

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 94
